observations.recommendation:
The Inventory Committee prioritize and ensure full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. 

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO/Municipal Treasurer maintain and update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards (PC) for proper monitoring and reconciliation.

The Inventory Committee together with the Appraisal/Disposal Committee prepare the IIRUP and process the disposal of unserviceable properties pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

Management upon the fulfillment or availability of all necessary requirements, drops the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts by drawing the following journal entry:

The Municipal Accountant submits the Schedule of Dormant Accounts as of December 31, 2023 and on the next quarters on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month and submit a request for the authority to write-off the dormant accounts following COA Circular No. 2023-008.

Management upon the approval of the request to drop the dormant account from the books of accounts the following journal entry:

Municipal Accountant records the transactions for the operations of the market and waterwork system in subsidiary ledgers maintained for these special accounts and to prepare the corresponding financial statements thereof to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balances as required under Section 105 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs.

The LDRRMO promptly submits the LDRRMFIP for CY 2023 and Monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction & Management Fund.

Management prioritizes the hiring of support staff for the LDRRM Office to assist the office in the implementation of the different LDRRM projects, programs and activities.

Management submits the necessary documents indicating therein the exact period during which the infrastructure projects commenced and concluded.

Management avoids awarding of pakyaw contracts to the same pakyaw labor group for two or more different pakyaw labor works having overlapping period of implementation.

Management complies with the supporting documents required in Item 4.0 of the GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006 dated December 6, 2006.

The Inventory Committee complies the necessary forms, registry, and reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 2022-004. 


The Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee  coordinate and ensure that accountabilities over these properties are established.

Management submits a formal letter to justify the inclusion of participants of employees whose functions are not directly related to the objectives of the activity.

Management submits the lacking documents and a copy of the re-entry action plans and/or any output in relation to the learning exchange activity.

The Municipal Accountant immediately trace and reconcile the CIP accounts and support a copy of a Certificate of Completion for the adjustment of each completed project. During the reconciliation, the Accounting Department may recognize the PPE account of those completed projects and the related depreciation must be computed therein.
The LDRRM Officer promptly submits the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

The Municipal Accountant and Agricultural Officer prepare and maintain the ledger cards and stock/property cards respectively, for the Breeding Stocks and reconcile their balances regularly. 

The Municipal Agriculture Officer (MAO) regularly monitor the condition of the livestock/breeding stocks under his supervision and update his records for easy reference. 

The inventory committee conduct physical count of the reported Breeding Stocks and submit the Report not later than January 31 of each year.

The Municipal Accountant sees to it that claims for repair has to be supported with records on repair history as required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Also, the Accounting Office reconstructs the maintenance/repair record of each vehicle owned and/or operated by the municipal government.

Management require the concerned personnel to submit the required supporting documents to COA for audit purposes and direct the concerned department head/personnel to submit justification for the noted lapses. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions be properly supported with complete documentation and in accordance with pertinent laws, rulesand regulations in order to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions and disbursements. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented shall be discontinued.

Management submit a valid justification letter for violating the pakyaw contract limit per project per pakyaw group. Moreover, management has to follow the revised guidelines for the implementation of infrastructure projects by administration set by the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

Management submit a valid justification letter for not preparing the APP basedon the required format. Moreover, management has to prepare the APP based on the approved format provided in the GPPB Circular 07-2015.

The Municipal Treasurer prepares a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books and eventually reported in the Financial Statements.

Management creates a task force to identify the lots owned by the LGU and review if the same are titled in the name of the LGU. Also, require the task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and ensure full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
1.10 Also, Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain and update the PPELC and PC for proper monitoring and reconciliation.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to promptly trace and reconcile the CIP accounts and provide a copy of the certificate of completion to support the adjustment of each completed project. During the reconciliation process, the Accounting Department shall recognize the PPE account for the completed projects, compute the related depreciation, and prepare the following journal entries:

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries for the aforementioned book reconciling items and stale checks to ensure an accurate balance of the Cash in Bank account as of year-end. Additionally, we recommended that properly reconciled BRS, complete with all necessary attachments and detailed information on outstanding checks, be submitted to the audit team for review.
Moving forward, we recommended the timely preparation and submission of monthly BRS, with the Municipal Accountant reviewing them for accuracy before forwarding them to the audit team.
Management assured compliance of the audit recommendations in the ensuing period.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare a RPCLRN using the prescribed format.
Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network and prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Furthermore, the General Services Office maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all the roads and their components.
Finally, ensure that the required disclosures are included in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
Management assured compliance of the audit recommendations in the ensuing period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Agricultural Officer prepare and maintain the ledger cards and stock/property cards respectively, for the Breeding Stocks and reconcile their balances regularly.
We further recommended that the MAO regularly monitor the condition of the livestock/breeding stocks under his supervision and update his records for easy reference.
Finally, we recommended that the inventory committee conduct a physical count of the reported Breeding Stocks and submit the Report not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that management immediately conduct a thorough review and reconciliation of the ₱650,000.00 fund transfers to various NGOs/POs.  For unimplemented projects, the municipality shall coordinate with recipient organizations to determine the reasons for non-implementation and take appropriate actions, including fund recovery if necessary, while for implemented but unliquidated projects, the municipality shall require the NGOs/POs to submit the required liquidation reports, supporting documents, and certifications of project completion within the prescribed timeframe. Henceforth, stringent compliance measures, including withholding future fund transfers to non-compliant organizations, shall be imposed to uphold financial integrity and ensure the timely and efficient use of public funds.
The Management will issue demand letters to the concerned NGOs/POs, thereby ensuring compliance with the audit recommendation in the ensuing period.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries for the current year as suggested below to correct the misstatements in the year-end financial statements relating to advance payments of real property taxes:
Moving forward, we recommended that management adopt the illustrated entries discussed above for recording advance payments in the ensuing years.
The management has already complied with the necessary adjusting entries as evidenced by JEV No. 100-2024-12-AJE02 (for General Fund) and JEV No. 200-2024-12-AJE01 (for SEF).
The audit team acknowledged the management's compliance with the audit recommendation through the preparation and recording of the necessary adjusting which appropriately corrected the misstatements in the current year's financial statements. However, we emphasize that the audit recommendation regarding the proper recognition and recording of advance payments in future periods, as illustrated and discussed in this audit observation, is yet to be implemented. Accordingly, we will continue to monitor and evaluate compliance in the ensuing audit period to ensure adherence to IPSAS 23 and relevant guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile with the records of the Petty Cash Custodian to ensure an accurate balance of the PCF account and ensure that subsequent replenishments are recorded in accordance with the proper accounting treatment, as outlined in Section 52 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adopt the suggested pro forma journal entry for recording Accounts Payable at year-end to minimize the risk of administrative penalties and ensure the accuracy of the financial statements:

We recommended that the concerned personnel submit the required supporting documents to COA for audit purposes. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions be properly supported with complete documentation and in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations in order to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions and disbursements.
The management submitted the copies of the PERT-CPM Network Diagram and Construction Schedule (Bar Chart and S-Surve) of the identified projects as mentioned in the AOM.
The audit team acknowledged the receipt of the documents; however, it is noted that several critical supporting documents remain lacking, such as signed Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed, As-Built Plans, detailed computations of contract time, properly dated and signed inspection and completion reports, and other required attachments. Partial submission does not satisfy this requirement and leaves the transactions in question open to doubt. We therefore reiterate the urgent need for the concerned personnel to submit the remaining required documents to facilitate a proper and conclusive audit evaluation.

Municipal Accountant reconciles the accounting records with the actual completed infrastructure projects and support a copy of a certificate of completion for the adjustment of each project and draw the following journal entries:

Municipal Accountant discloses in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the balances of Unexpended LDRRMF as shown in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for fair presentation and to enhance the understandability of the users of Financial Statements.

Municipal Accountant records the funds received with specific purpose to Due to NGAs account in the Trust Fund with corresponding transfers of fund to the Cash in Bank account in the Trust Fund. Also, any unspent balances from LGSF-FA 2022 shall be transferred and accurately recorded in the Trust Fund.

The Municipal Accountant records the purchases of supplies and materials using the perpetual inventory method pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Volume I Manual and the Municipal Treasurer/GSO prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit it to the Municipal Accountant which shall be used as basis in recording the issuances/expense account as required under Section 121 of the same manual. For the unutilized inventories at year end, Management has also agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to conduct a physical inventory and the Municipal Accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the municipality.

Management designates another qualified Special Disbursing Officer if additional cash advances are necessary and justified under the circumstances and/or increase the amount of fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer to sufficiently cover her cash accountabilities.

Municipal Accountant records liquidation of cash advance for confidential/intelligence expenses only upon receipt of a copy of the transmittal of documents duly acknowledged by the ICFAU pursuant to Item 6.3.3.e of COA-DBM-DILG-GC for GOCCs-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 in order to fairly present the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and Confidential Expenses in the financial statements.

We also recommend the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that savings from the approved local budget has been approved by the Sanggunian before effecting payment chargeable to the General Fund.

We recommend that the BAC should minimize the practice of piecemeal procurement of goods by small value procurement and prepare the appropriate Consolidated Annual Procurement Plan to guide the agency in the procurement of goods and services. Hence, procurement of goods and services by small value procurement could be minimized.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor to strictly implement the use of the biometric system in monitoring the time and attendance of the employees to achieve the intended purpose of the system. Also, direct the Human Resource Management Officer to require all employees to submit the manually prepared DTR completely filled up and strictly in accordance with the machine generated time records and at the same time maintain an updated logbook that will be regularly checked and inspected. If the employee is on field or official travel, require the employee to support the DTR with locator slips or travel order and certificate of appearance and in case the employee is on leave, attach the approved leave application in the DTR.

a)	Require the Municipal Budget Officer/Municipal Accountant to charge/record wages of casual/contractual employees assigned in the different offices of the municipality to its appropriate accounts, Salaries and Wages- Casual (705) and Salaries and Wages – Contractual (706) as provided for in the Chart of Accounts under the NGAS. 

b) Maximize the utilization of manpower in order not to exceed the 45% budgetary limitation for personal services.

Enforce immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances and/or refund the excess or unutilized portion of said cash advance, if any, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salary or any amount due them and no additional cash advance shall be granted until full liquidation of the previous cash advances is made.

We recommend that management:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to settle/liquidate Trust Receipts to agencies concerned as soon as the purpose for which it was granted had already been served or completed and return any unused balance thereof as required under COA Circular No. 94-013.

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to revert to Retained Operating Surplus by certifying that these accounts have been outstanding for over two (2) years period, in cases that these accounts are balances of completed/accomplished projects/programs or return the same to the source agency, depending on the Memorandum of Agreement provision that was signed by the parties.
